title: Memory Management
description: Understand ARC, weak references, and avoiding retain cycles in Swift.

sections.title: Memory Management Demo
sections.expectedOutput:
Creating first person...
Alice initialized

Creating second person and linking...
Bob initialized

Releasing person1...
Alice deallocated

Releasing person2...
Bob deallocated

Done - both should be deallocated

sections.question: When should you use 'weak' instead of strong reference?
options: When the referenced object may be deallocated while this object exists
sections.explanation: Use 'weak' when the referenced object might be deallocated before this object, preventing retain cycles.
